1:
People born on this day are creative and inventive natures. They hold firmly to their views and possess immense powers of persuasion.
The first of all numbers is the foundation upon which everything rests. For this reason it stands as the head of the family of numbers.
They do not much care for restrictions or unsolicited advice.
They prefer to work alone, independently of others, whether equals or subordinates. All of this combined makes them mini- (and sometimes maxi-) dictators.
These people are highly ambitious and possess tremendous determination and willpower, which in action manifests as stubbornness and self-will.
A child born on the 1st will not rest until its demand is met, and to have its own way it will turn everything upside down.
Such people require tactful handling. People born on this day rise to the very top of the career ladder if the lines of the palm align with the results of numerological analysis.

The dominant emotion of these people is excessive ambition, and to fulfil it they direct all their mental faculties.
This naturally leaves little time for love and romance.
If an emotional attachment does arise, it is fleeting, secondary, and always subordinate to the main goal: the satisfaction of ambition.
Thus the object of their affection must be submissive, self-effacing, and undemanding. Sex too is one-sided for such people, transient, and aimed at self-gratification.
Concern for the partner is hardly regarded as of any importance.
For this reason marriage is rarely successful for such people, unless the partner shows understanding and respect for the extreme qualities of the individuality of one born on the first.

As stated above, the satisfaction of ambition is the main goal in the life of these people. Such people show firm determination and resilience, and do not abandon what they have set out to do. All of this is well and good if tempered by good judgement, which such people rarely possess.

The most fortunate partners are those born on the 1st: like attracts like; also those born on the 10th, 19th, 28th (digit sum 1; zero, though it carries meaning, does not affect the numerology); 4th, 13th, 22nd, 31st (digit sum 4).
Disharmonious relations with those born on the 2nd, 11th, 20th, 29th (digit sum 2); 6th, 15th, 24th (digit sum 6); 7th, 16th, 25th (digit sum 7).
Sunday, Monday. If these fall on the 10th, 19th, or 28th, the effect is doubled.

Topaz, yellow diamond, and all stones of these shades (worn in contact with the body).
Gold (its weight must be no less than 1.2 g, otherwise it will have no effect; worn in contact with the body).
Golden, light yellow; an item of the same colour as the metal or stone should be worn at all times.
Heart, eyes; high blood pressure is possible, so one should be examined more often.

Numerologically, the number 1 is one of the very best. People born on this day are outstanding personalities, full of firm determination and creative ability.
Personal qualities of the highest order, yet such a person experiences enormous difficulty in relations with others. (Those around them must show understanding, for people of the number 1 are not cruel by nature, merely careless in their dealings.)
10:
Zero is a number that softens the one preceding it. Therefore the number 10 is a weakened number 1.
These people are leaders, but less dominant than those born on the 1st. They possess all the qualities of the 1, but without the excess.
These people are ambitious and think clearly. They waste no time in carrying out their plans and tolerate no interference.
They are stubborn and firm and do not stop until they have accomplished everything they set out to do. They are usually successful in life, for they possess all the positive qualities of the number 1, but are less abrupt and not so domineering.
Despite their success, they cannot reach the heights attained by those born on the 1st.
They are intelligent, possess imagination, creative ability, and courage. They can make great wagers to achieve success and do not fear losses, all the more so since success usually comes to them.

Being leaders by nature, such people require partners who will accept their actions without unnecessary questions.
In this they resemble those born on the 1st, who need submissive partners.
In matters of sex they are very strong, and they need equally strong partners, since physical satisfaction is very important to them.
However, unlike those born on the 1st, the partner need not submit too strongly. As long as they are not led by the nose, they get on well with people.

The chief issue is a certain insensitivity towards subordinates. Moreover, they are quick-tempered. If they encounter inertia, they cannot bear it, since they are themselves very energetic.
In doing so they make enemies, yet they still disregard the opinions of others. They may find themselves isolated, but they care little about it.
If left unchecked, they will sweep everything from their path. Such people cannot tolerate those who fail to do what they are capable of.
They will inevitably remember the shortcomings of others, brand them as incapable, and hold them in contempt. They need to cultivate tolerance within themselves.

These people are very sincere by nature and expect sincerity from others.
They are good friends and devoted lovers, but they demand the same of their partners. They are independent, and their chief motto is: live and let live.
They do not like to be followed, but they do not demand blind obedience, only recognition of their leadership and their ability to lead.
They also meddle in nothing, love to work alone, yet at the same time they need to be admired.
They achieve success in life by being intelligent, energetic, and enterprising. They are not difficult to get along with. This is a very positive and fortunate number.
Heart and eyes, high blood pressure.

The number 10 is a very good number. These people can attain a high position. They are intolerant of others and cannot bear to see anyone do work worse than they would.
They need to try to overcome this weakness. Because of their ambition they often suffer and are always in a state of tension.
They work in such a way that they can drive themselves to exhaustion. They need to refrain from excess, both in physical activity and in ambition and emotion.
Above all, they should cultivate patience, since they acquire enemies through their hot temper. Otherwise, this is a good number, and people of the 10 are happy and fortunate.
11:
Its components and the sum of its two digits are opposed to one another.
The number 11 is two ones, and so it possesses the doubled qualities of the number 1, but the sum of these digits is 2, which is diametrically opposite, which is why people of the 11 are very difficult to understand.
They are highly ambitious and cannot be content with second place. They are energetic, enterprising, very individual, and independent.
They make firm decisions. These are positive characters, very bold, but beneath it all lies sensitivity and delicacy.
They plan magnificently and execute their plans brilliantly, if they act at once. The moment hesitation appears, they are lost. They devise bold plans, but often lack the courage to bring them to life.
They wish to impose their will on others, but are too gentle by nature to do so. They fall into conflict because of their desire to express their views freely and, at the same time, their excessive sensitivity to the feelings of others.
They must have their say, but they do it in the manner of pinpricks, instead of speaking everything out plainly.
Their inability to come out into the open and fight as they would wish drives them to despair, and so they become first-class grumblers.
They will "peck" instead of "shaking out" everything at once.
In general, they are very sensitive, but since the number includes two ones, they are often utterly unpredictable. Sometimes they may say too much, and at other times they are silent.
When and what they will do, no one knows. Their colossal ambition makes them want more and more for themselves, but often their desires exceed their possibilities.
It is here that failure lies in wait for them. Having the digit sum 2, these people cannot reconcile themselves to defeat, but they lack the strength to fight.
The slightest failure is a catastrophe for them. While all goes well, they are very positive people, but at the first signs of failure or opposition they break down and fall into deep despondency and pessimism.

In emotional and sexual life they are likewise contradictory. They possess great appetite and sexual power. The trouble begins at the slightest difficulty.
They are too shy to seek the satisfaction of their desires and, at the same time, extremely sincere and devoted.
They cannot endure betrayal any more than they can defeat. And if they do not meet a suitable partner, they will be broken for the rest of their lives.
Shy and lacking in confidence, they nonetheless tolerate no interference, and an offer of support they take as an insult.
They seek independence, but are incapable of it. They have the desire for independence, but lack the strength to achieve it.
In general, in such people a constant struggle takes place between the strong traits of the number 1 and the gentle, dependent character of the number 2, as a result of which they are restless and unhappy.

The fundamental trait of the number 11 is the struggle of ambition with insecurity. These people possess the intellect necessary for success, but they lack a strong backbone and energy.
They need support, which at the same time they reject as weakness. They would like to command, but are too shy to assert themselves.
Indeed, they are a tangle of contradictions. They need to learn to analyse themselves and to develop their positive qualities.
If they do not do this, the success they seek will slip away from them. The main thing for them is to learn to bring their ideas to life, for they possess the intellect of both the 1 and the 2, and they need to stop dreaming and act.

Everyone who deals with the number 11 must be a monument of patience and tact. One should provide the support the number 11 desperately needs without letting it show.
If such a person senses that they are the weaker one, they immediately reject this support.
The friends and spouses of such people must be able to be leaders when necessary, and followers when required. The main thing is to know when to lead and when to follow.
Moreover, the partner must be intelligent and able to commune on a spiritual level. These people must be constantly encouraged and not allowed to fall into despondency or despair.
They are very difficult because of their unpredictability, and their partners must always be a step ahead in order to sense what is required of them.
With these people endless patience and tact, devotion and sincerity are needed. If the paths must part, it should be done as gently as possible.
Among people of the 11th there are very many who take their own lives, caused by the constant conflict between ambition and possibility.

5, 15, 23. The number 5 is especially hostile because of the mercenary nature of its character. Such a partner can easily cast aside their partner born on the 11th, for whom such an act would mean complete ruin.
Therefore those born on the 11th should avoid all involvement with those born on the 5th. Other hostile numbers: 4, 13, 22, 31.
The number 4 is a pessimist and can never provide the support the number 11 needs, but can only undermine what little self-belief there is in it.

The number 11 is a good number. But these people should be warned against despondency, and they should develop self-confidence; they should beware of inactivity and be ready to put their plans into effect.
Missed opportunities disappoint them and plunge them into despondency.
They should develop patience and restrain their ambitions. Such people need to beware of foolish desires and remain within certain bounds.
This is, of course, easier said than done, and here the help of loved ones is needed, who must tactfully and diplomatically steer them away from unattainable desires onto a path where there is more chance of success. They need to remember the saying: "Rome was not built in a day."
12:
Courage, ambition, and the positive qualities of a leader. These people possess intellect, energy, and somewhat resemble a bulldozer.
On the other hand, the number 2 is a gentle, romantic dreamer, insecure, requiring constant approval, very intelligent but not energetic.
The sum of these two digits is 3, also a very strong number, representing an intensified number 1. In the number 3 the leader turns into a dictator, and ambition into a thirst for power.
The result is a positive character, beneath which lie insecurity and gentleness.
The sensitivity of the number 2 shows itself in that such people can be persuaded, not coerced by force.
This number is a good subordinate, provided the leader possesses tact and diplomacy.
Bulldozer methods are unsuitable here, while with love one can achieve anything. The insecurity can be overcome by the confidence of the numbers 1 and 3; such people will always consult others before acting.
They need encouragement. They possess the intellect of the number 2 and the practical mind of the number 1.
They are disciplined like the number 3, and systematically methodical like the 1. They make excellent commanders, but unlike the number 3, they are not rough with their subordinates. They strive to rule with consent.
This is a very good number, since it possesses all the positive qualities of the numbers 1 and 3. The shortcomings of the number 2 are balanced out.

They are easy to live with. They need the approval of their colleagues, but they do not seek such support as the number 2 does.
They possess strength of character without pedantry and fanaticism. In their attachments they are sincere and wish to have equal partners.
These people make good marriages, provided their partners do not have an excess of the same shortcomings as they do.
They are sexually strong and have no inner inhibitions.
They expect the same participation from their partners, who must be free of prejudices and taboos. They are strong and love to experiment. Their sensitivity from the number 2 makes them tender lovers, and spiritual satisfaction is also important to them.
Though they are sensual, they will never obtain full satisfaction unless they have spiritual communion with their partner. To obtain satisfaction they need intellectual communication.

Their sensitivity sometimes reaches the point where they cannot forgive and forget. A real or imagined offence troubles their mind for a long time. It will fester within them and may make them unhappy.
Insecurity prevents them from settling matters directly. All of this makes them unhappy. Moreover, they are quick-tempered and become irritated if someone tries to give them advice.
They need the approval of those around them, and they do not like being told an opinion with which they disagree.
Here the influence of the number 3 shows itself. With such people tact is necessary. When faced with difficulties, they lose confidence and the ability to continue what they have begun.
Here the traits of the number 2 appear, and those around them must approve of them without letting it show.

These people get on well with others. They are usually popular and are good companions thanks to their intellect and positive character. They are sensitive to other people and try to offend no one.
When necessary, they can speak their whole mind.
In other words, you always know what to expect from them. They are well-balanced and do not have the complexes of the number 11, owing to which they can get on well with most people.
high blood pressure, stomach disorders, skin diseases

This is a very good number, and such people are very well-balanced. But they are insecure, and at times the dreamer prevails in them.
They should try to overcome these traits. In addition, they need to develop patience and understand that life has its good and bad sides.
If their plans run into obstacles, they should not abandon them. Things cannot always be good, and instead of giving up, these people should keep on fighting.
Otherwise this is a very good number, and people of the 12 are, as a rule, fortunate. The second half of their life is better than the first.
13:
It is considered unlucky, but this is mistaken and based on superstition.
The numbers 1 and 3 are very strong and signify success in one's career. They are practical, plan well, and carry out their plans energetically and systematically.
They are intelligent, creative, and have quite definite views. Independent, proud, quick to pay their debts.
They are ambitious, very firm, and always finish what they have started. The combination of these two numbers is simply astonishing.
Now let us consider the sum, 4, which is directly opposite to 1 and 3. Here there is innate pessimism, constant despondency. These two contradictory tendencies produce an extremely unhappy state of mind.
On the one hand, powerful ambition, a desire for success in climbing the career ladder, and on the other, insecurity, pessimism, the rejection of chances, however reliable they may be.
Lost opportunities prevent the attainment of success, and one can imagine the disappointment that ensues. The number 13 possesses a tenacious mind and the ability to grasp everything.
These people learn quickly, are extremely systematic and methodical. They are highly efficient and do their work with a minimum of effort.
One might suppose that, possessing such qualities, the number 13 would always attain success, but this is not so.
Such people are doomed to failure unless they take themselves in hand. They are rarely content with what they have, and always want more. In this the ambition of the numbers 1 and 3 shows itself.

The emotional and sexual life of the number 13 is complex and difficult. Once again there is a conflict of two opposing numbers.
On the surface the number 13 is confident, but this is only on the surface. Inwardly such people crave encouragement, and they need someone who can give it to them.
They must be nudged onward all the time. The number 4 plays a very large role in their emotional life.
The number 4 does not attach itself easily, but once attached, it remains devoted for life, and so the sincerity of the number 13 is beyond doubt. Strangely enough, in sex emotion plays a very small role, if it matters at all.
They are very sexual and possess a Pantagruelian appetite, but are devoid of feeling.
Even if they are with a beloved person, it is all purely physical and serves only to satisfy animal desire. Desire arises in them very quickly and in its strength is almost uncontrollable.

The chief shortcoming of the number 13 is extreme pessimism. This can nullify all its positive qualities.
These people are also jealous because of their lack of self-confidence.
Though not quarrelsome by nature, they can make enemies, since their pessimism irritates others.
They are never content with what they have; even if they have everything one could dream of, they always want more. This can lead them to disappointment and despondency.

The number 13 is a very difficult partner, and for its happiness it needs a partner who possesses the qualities of many different people.
First and foremost, they need support and confidence. Morally they are always in constant sorrow, so their partner must be able to dispel such thoughts and steer them towards more positive thinking.
Despite this, the number 13 is a leader by instinct and a commander by nature. Their partners must be able to follow them.
The easiest path for the friends and spouses of the number 13 is to help it develop within itself those many fine qualities of mind and soul that it possesses.
High spiritual qualities, efficiency, and devotion make them outstanding people.
Liver, and also back pain and headaches

The number 13 needs to overcome pessimism and look on the bright sides of life.
Such people are the fortunate possessors of good qualities and should develop them in place of the negative ones. Success is very close to them, and they must not drive it away by missing opportunities.
They possess the qualities of leaders and commanders, and they are serious as well.
There is nothing that would stand in the way of success in their life. The only shortcoming is a negative attitude towards everything they do.
They must overcome this by applying positive possibilities and willpower.
14:
The two digits are opposed, while their sum, 5, represents an entirely different character, but there is no great conflict here.
1 is the leader, hot-tempered and ambitious; 4 is very intelligent, methodical, a pessimist and a spendthrift; the sum of these digits is a mercenary number whose one god is money.
This is the only combination of its kind.
They unite ambition and greed for money. Such people are very successful in material matters, since they possess the gift of leadership, initiative and energy, intellect, and can direct all their energy towards acquiring money.
They are, however, extravagant, but this quality is kept in check by the digit 5.
Beneath it all lies the pessimism characteristic of the 4, but this is nonetheless not a very dominant trait. On the whole, the 14 is an ambitious, money-oriented, determined, and very fortunate person.

The spouses and friends of such people must be wealthy, otherwise they have nothing to count on in the way of good relations.
These are not very warm people, and they are not easy to love. On the other hand, they have the attractive qualities of the 1, and the opposite sex is drawn to them.
They are courteous and diplomatic. On the surface they have many friends; they turn no one against themselves, since they never know when a person may come in useful.
Their spouses must be equally materialistic. A person of a different cast of mind does not get on with them. They have no need of people who are not interested in money.
This is their most habitual medium of communication. Sexually they are strong people, but here too they are interested in the opportunity to lay hold of money.

The 14 is an opportunist, using people and then discarding them when no longer needed, and so acquires enemies. Being something of a pessimist, suspicious. Such people are extremists and rush from one extreme to another.

Despite success in material matters, such people are lonely; they drive away friends with their bare materialism. This must be fought against. They have negative thinking, which makes life with them difficult. They need to think more positively and reinforce their good qualities.
15:
A leader, intelligent and decisive; the number 5 is Mercury, the planet of business and finance.
The sum of these digits is 6, a very romantic number, the planet Venus, which governs love and relations with the opposite sex.
This is a magnetic personality, drawing the opposite sex as a flame draws moths.
On the whole the 15 is a leader, mercenary, a businessperson and a romantic. A very interesting combination. The mercenary quality in this combination is suppressed by the romanticism of the number 6.
The 15 is a very persistent number and, having made a decision, sticks to it, whether good or bad. This persistence shows itself in work as well: they will never leave anything unfinished.
Such people rarely continue work they have abandoned: they hand it over to the direction of others, while they themselves take up something new.
The chief traits are the romanticism of the 6. Materialism is present, but it is diluted. Such a person seeks a romantic nature.

If the partner has money, so much the better, but this is not as pronounced as for the number 5. These people are easily excitable, and easily angered. They cool down as quickly as they flare up.
People by whom they are not captivated spiritually and romantically will not suit them. The 15 is an incurable romantic.
They love money, but its absence does not trouble them too much. The 15 gets on easily with people, and best of all with those who are well provided for. Their marriages are happy, but their spouses must respond to their romanticism, for this is the main thing for them.
The spouse must be able to live in the romantic world that a person of the number 15 builds for themselves, and must on no account destroy it.

The number 15 easily falls under influence, and under a bad influence may fall into bad company or become the victim of unscrupulous people.
Their love of money may lead them into gambling and unlawful activity. They are excitable and hot-tempered.

This is a peaceful number, and these people are always materially prosperous. But before beginning anything, they should think it over carefully, since they go off course.
They should think their decisions through themselves. They need to learn patience and not be carried away by trifles. The number is peaceful, but not entirely mercenary. These people can have success in life.
16:
The number 1 is the leader, ambitious, successful. It carries out its plans and tolerates no contradiction. It is intellect, careful planning. It is the commander, compelling unquestioning obedience.
The presence of the number 6 lends a leaning towards romance and sentimentality, a sensitivity to nature, to the symbols of romance. People born on the 16th become good artists and writers, if they can occupy themselves with one pursuit for long.
The sum of the two digits is 7, which signifies changeability, a love of travel, restlessness. The combination of 6 and 7 makes these people good explorers.
The number 1 gives them courage and energy for adventure. These people are interesting and possess a captivating personality.
They are very attractive to the opposite sex, and women are never short of admirers. They have deep emotions, but at the same time they possess great independence, and they are difficult to intimidate.

These people are capable of strong attachment, but they cannot be with one person for long; they need change. Yet their attachment does not diminish.
In their feelings they remain constant, but they merely need something different for a time.
Sexually they are strong and aggressive. Men will persistently lay siege to the object of their adoration with a force that is hard to resist. But he is not an animal, as it might seem. These people are good lovers, and the objects of their passion are truly fortunate.
In friendship they are devoted and, thanks to their personality, have many friends. They are diplomatic and generous, and this makes them the life of the party.

These people have a tendency to wander. This occurs under the influence of the number 7. They cannot stay with one pursuit or one person for long.
They are like a rolling tumbleweed. If they are insufficiently understood, they end up isolated. They begin to flit from one job to another, from one person to another.

These people are happy with those who respect their independence and do not try to interfere with them or restrict them. In this the influence of the number 1 shows itself. Their romantic inclinations require for them a spouse who can be sentimental without lapsing into melodrama.
The partner must be sensitive to their mood, equally active in sex, with the same enthusiasm, without inhibitions.
These are free-thinking people, and almost nothing shocks them. They expect the same of others. In this the influence of the number 1 shows itself.

These people need to cultivate stability within themselves, otherwise they may end up rootless. If they must roam, they should see to a place to which they can return. Otherwise the 16 is a good number.
17:
Here too the number 1 lends ambition, energy, and independence, a creative character and the precise execution of plans. The number 7 introduces change, restlessness, a love of wandering, fidgetiness.
In combination with the 1 the result is a good writer or artist. At the same time, the artist within these people is a practical person, with an intelligent and logical approach to a problem.
The sum, 8, gives a split personality that is misunderstood. As a result, the 17 has three main traits: the ambition and energy of the 1, the changeability and the difficulty of understanding of the number 8.

Emotionally these people are changeable under the influence of the number 7, but the number 1 makes them devoted, for in their lasting bonds they are faithful, despite minor infidelities. Their partners must understand this. If the partner tries to restrict them or hinder them in anything, they may go to a complete rupture.
They may enter into marriage more than once, and their later marriages are better than the earlier ones.
Sexually this is the strongest number. They love to experiment and have no inhibitions, are strong and powerful in sex, but rough. Their partners must be strong.

The very changeability of their nature, which makes them interesting people, often brings them unhappiness.
They cannot be constant either in work or in their relationships with people, unless this is connected with travel and change. This is why their career is rarely successful, despite their abilities. It is best for them to have an occupation that gives them freedom of movement.

The number 7 plays the dominant role in their relations with people. They are happy with someone who lets them be themselves and does not interfere with their leading functions. On no account should their freedom be restricted.
These people are interesting, intelligent, and energetic. They need to cultivate stability and curb an overly great craving for change.
18:
An unusual combination arises of the ambitious, strong, and energetic number 1 with the misunderstood number 8, which is very talented, artistic, and sensitive.
In combination with the 1 this produces an outstanding personality. Such a combination may prove explosive, but the number 9 balances out this tendency.
Since 9 is the planet Mars, it lends fighting qualities. Thus the properties of all three numbers find their reflection in the number 18.

The emotional and sexual characteristics of the number 18 are mainly governed by the number 9. These people are very sexual, but cannot express their desire and often suffer disappointment. The expression of desire seems to them shameful and a sign of weakness. Instead they suppress themselves into despondency.
But here too there is the influence of the number 8, representing a tender and sensitive lover who is moreover sexually strong. Conflict is very rare, since the number 8 hides nothing from those it loves.
All the components, 1, 8, 9, are sexually strong, and one can imagine the result.
The number 9 dominates, as does the number 1, so the number 18 strives to take the upper hand in matters of the heart, otherwise nothing will come of it, and the partner must be gentle and yielding.
Moreover, he must play the leading role in sex because of the number 9, since the number 18 cannot openly express its feelings and desires.
In friendship this number is devoted in every way, but it is not easy to get on with. At the same time the number 8 softens the dominant character of the number 18 and prevents it from forcibly imposing its decisions on others.
Though the number 18 is not easy to get on with, such people are interesting companions, being intelligent and positive. In short, the partner or friend of such a person must be yielding and at the same time be a leader in sex, that is, must know when one should take the lead.
This number is very dominant and commanding; only the presence of the number 8 keeps it from being unpleasant.

The friends of the number 18 can be those who possess the same spiritual qualities and ambition. Those born on the 18th cannot understand a lack of ambition; it is their driving force, and they consider people without it to be weaklings.
Therefore they cannot deal with them. They slightly despise those who do not possess the same energy, but they can tolerate them, and in this the influence of the number 8 shows itself.
Despite their dominant nature, they can be good friends, since the number 8 gives them sensitivity and restrains their urge to command. In the interests of friendship one should not contradict them too much.

These people must be gentler with those around them, otherwise they will lose the friends they have.
They possess the splendid qualities of leaders—intellect, industriousness—but when they begin to treat others without ceremony, they become unpopular. Not everyone can understand them as their loved ones do.
They should not be intolerant of those who disagree with them. If they overcome these negative qualities and tendencies, they can become outstanding people and rise to great heights.
19:
Here the 1 is repeated twice—in the number itself and in the digit sum. Therefore these people will never submit to anyone.
Self-assurance—a trait characteristic of the number 1—is carried in them to the point of vanity. They consider themselves first in everything and will never agree that anyone could do work better than they. The whole world revolves around them.
They are never content with what they have; they always need more. They possess a strong personality and can bend the opinions of others to their own way solely by the force of their personality. Hence their thirst for power.
They can destroy anything that stands in the way of their ambition, and in doing so they will not be squeamish in their means, even if murder is required. Determination and energy are the chief traits of the numbers 1 and 9, and in the number 19 they are present in triple measure.

The same may be said of their emotional and sexual life, where they must likewise dominate. It does not occur to them that another might have feelings to be reckoned with, since they have no such feelings themselves.
Those around them are only instruments serving their purpose. They are exceptionally selfish and can push a partner away when they no longer need them.
Emotions play no great role for them and may manifest only in early youth and in late old age.
Whatever attachments arise in them, they will be lonely. Sexually it is impossible to satisfy them, because they always lack something.
Under the influence of the number 9 they cannot express their desires, and so the guidance of a partner is necessary, but if the partner does not respond, the number 19 will not stop short of using force.
By every account, this is a very dangerous number. Such people are very sexual, and considering that for them there are no barriers when they want something, they should at times be feared.

Terrible ambition and a domineering character can lead to ruin. They force their way forward, sweeping aside all resistance, casting off friends and enemies alike, until not a single friend remains to them.
They may attain success, but since they never have enough of anything, their insatiable thirst for power may turn to failure for them.

Like the number 5, the number 19 is in harmony with its own kind. They are very dominant, and those who are bound to them will most likely get on with them out of fear rather than affection.
On the other hand, those who are dear to them will submit to them in everything and will be devoted, but it is best if these people too are born on this number.
For all their dominance, these people are capable of inspiring complete devotion to themselves; they possess a magnetic personality and by this win others over. Their spouses must be either equals or must submit completely: either equals or slaves.

1, 10, 28; 9, 18, 27; 5, 14, 23
No other numbers can be harmonious, and all are hostile.

It is hardly possible to recommend anything to this number, because they accept no recommendations. As far as possible they should restrain their fury and their ambitions.
They possess the dynamic qualities of a leader and in this have no equals, but then they become greedy for power, and at this point they begin to appropriate what belongs to others and to commit unlawful things in order to keep it.
They reach dizzying heights, and then drop like a stone into the very depths of humiliation, and the blame for this lies with themselves. If only they could be content with what they will undoubtedly attain, brilliance awaits them not only in their own time but in history.
But if they do enter history, it is as dictators who were thrown out by the very people over whom they wished to hold sway.
2:
The number 2 is the opposite of the number 1. It is, as it were, feminine, gentle, and romantic.
Those born on this day are intelligent, creative people; they possess artistic abilities, a lively and rich imagination.
Their strength lies in their mind, and in all things they seek spiritual rather than physical satisfaction.
In short, they are dreamers, living in spiritual clouds of their own making, rather than planning a material, worldly life.
Such people are gentle and indulgent by nature, and they lack the strength and assertiveness to advance in life. They are also not very strong and avoid hard labour.

These people seek spiritual communion with their partner. Their love is born in the mind, and they do not recognise physical intimacy without a spiritual bond.
Therefore sexual power is rather the exception in them.

They are proud and do not like to be beholden to anyone. Both are firm and do not give up to the last. Where, then, is the complexity?
It is in the sum—4, which is completely opposite: this is a born pessimist. Such people live in a state of complete despondency.
Thus, on the one hand, all-conquering ambition, a desire for success and a high career, and on the other—insecurity and pessimism, indecision when chances arise, however safe they may be. Between these components a great conflict arises, something like a split personality, though not in the way it is with the number 8.

The emotional life of the number 31 is complex. The conflict of the two numbers plays a large role in it.
On the surface the number 31 is confident and outstanding; within there exists a certain insecurity, almost imperceptible. Such people are very much in need of approval, they need someone who could provide it.
The number 4 plays a large role in their emotional life. This number reacts slowly to emotions, but is distinguished by lifelong devotion.
The sincerity of such people is beyond doubt. They do not fall in love easily, nor do they love easily. In sex it is a different picture. Here they are utterly unemotional.
They are very sexual and possess a great appetite, but feelings play no great role in it. Even if they love, the act is purely physical, and they are concerned only with their own animal desires, which come in a surge and are uncontrolled. If they cannot quench their thirst, they become nervous and irritable.

The chief one is pessimism, which can cross out all their good qualities. Moreover, they are jealous, since they easily lose self-confidence. They are always dissatisfied with what they have. Even if they have everything one could dream of, they want something more. This can lead to disappointment.

They have a complex character, and only very many-sided personalities can make them happy. Their state of mind inclines towards sorrow and despondency, though they rarely show it.
Their partner must be able to sense and dispel such gloomy thoughts and divert their attention to more pleasant things.
Despite all this, these people are leaders by instinct and commanders by nature, and their partners must follow them. Those around them must help them develop the many fine qualities of mind and heart that they possess.
Their splendid mind, efficiency, and devotion make them outstanding personalities. Their partners will be happy if they can understand their needs.
Heart (this comes from a leaning towards pessimism), the heart should be checked more often

They need to overcome their leaning towards pessimism and look on the bright sides of things. They are the fortunate possessors of splendid qualities, and they should develop them instead of suppressing them, as they often do. Success is close to them, and they must not miss opportunities. They possess the qualities of leaders and are very serious. In addition, they should develop willpower.
4:
Connected with the Sun and the number 1, and in occultism it is written as 4-1.
People of the number 4 have a peculiar character; they look at everything from the opposite point of view.
In all arguments and discussions they take the opposing position. And though they do not wish to become quarrelsome, they nonetheless provoke opposition and in doing so make for themselves many hidden enemies who constantly work against them.
They instinctively rebel against rules and customs. And if they manage to have their way, they turn the order of things around, even in society and government.
They often rebel against constitutional authority and create new rules. They are drawn to social questions and reforms of every kind, and they are very stubborn in their opinions.
The chief characteristic of these people is intellect and pessimism. These people usually make a brilliant academic career.
They have very keen powers of observation and a good receptivity for study.
They are very efficient and good workers, but without excessive zeal. Their innate intellect helps them do their work systematically and methodically, without dashing about.
They are not hot-tempered and very rarely quarrel. They are spendthrifts. Money leaves them as quickly as it comes.

The number 4 does not become captivated easily, but if it does become captivated, it is for life. Such people are faithful not out of obligation, but because it is very difficult for them to fall in love.
They possess an average sexual appetite, but they can by no means be called weak. They are sexually moderate and experience no great difficulty in finding a partner.
At the same time, they are born pessimists and live in constant sorrow.
This is very hard on those around them, since it is very difficult to be near pessimists and insecure people.
These two traits lead to a very unpleasant emotion—the green-eyed monster—jealousy.
Not believing in themselves overmuch, they need constant approval, and if they do not receive it, they become prickly and hot-tempered.
With all this, they feel good if there is someone nearby who supports them.

Despite their many positive qualities, the number 4 rarely attains great success in life because of an all-crossing-out pessimism.
Because of a tendency always to see the dark sides and a lack of confidence, they often miss good opportunities.
Instead of trying their utmost and seizing the chance, they do nothing at all, believing that nothing will come of it anyway.
They lack the strength to fight failures, and, so as not to suffer defeat, they prefer to undertake nothing.
THE NUMBER 4 IS A SPENDTHRIFT.
They have no idea how to economise, and in times of need they are extremely dejected if they cannot find the means for their expenses.
This, in turn, brings them even greater disappointment, and their insecurity grows.

The partners and friends of the number 4 must encourage them, and they must always have someone to lean on.
Deprived of support, they become lost and sink even deeper into the sea of uncertainty created by themselves.
All those around them must be the embodiment of patience and strength, because it is very difficult to live with a person who is constantly in need of moral support.
In return, however, one can receive impeccable devotion, because such people do not attach themselves easily at all, but, once attached, they keep it forever and do not abandon their partner.
Aquamarine (white and dark sapphires, which should be worn on the body).
Light blue or white. All light tones, electric blue and grey.
Kidneys, back pain, and headaches. One must beware of spicy food.

People of the number 4 will never be happy in this material world. Despite their impeccable devotion to partners and friends, they are very difficult to live with because of their pessimism.
Very often they push away the very people they desperately need.
They are like spiritual parasites and feed off those around them until they have sucked all the emotion out of them and cast them aside.
After this they feel lost and betrayed, not realising that they themselves are the cause of this "betrayal."
Missing opportunities, they always remain behind, and instead of analysing the reason, they inwardly blame the whole world for injustice.
They must at all costs overcome the feeling of inferiority from which they suffer, because most often the cause of failure lies in themselves.
They should not lean on others and should believe in themselves. They need to develop their best qualities, instead of falling into pessimism and bewailing their own dear selves.
Few people are as methodical as they are. And their efficiency is very high.
If they would analyse themselves as methodically as they do their work, they would be far happier and would attain success, being intelligent and capable.
5:
People born on the 5th are mercenary; the acquisition and possession of money constitutes the main goal of their life.
They are adroit and possess good business qualities. They are usually successful in business and gather large sums in short periods.
Because of their love of money and their impatience to acquire it, they often break the law. Being intelligent and very inventive, they direct all their energy into finding ways and means to enrich themselves.
These people possess many good qualities. For example, they think quickly and make decisions. They waste no time in constructing plans and prefer firm steps.
They are easy to get on with, because they are restrained in speech and born diplomats.
By nature they are resilient and quickly recover from the heaviest blows of fate, provided it does not touch their wealth.
All their positive qualities are directed towards one goal—financial success.
Intellect, adroitness, diplomacy, tact, decisiveness, swiftness of action in carrying out plans—all these qualities are used by them to the last drop, if the goal is money. They are not very good at anything else.
People of the number 5 possess high intelligence. They are easily excitable and live on their nerves all the time. They think quickly and arrive at decisions. They are impulsive in their actions.
They lack the patience for painstaking labour.
They have a "nose" for ideas and inventions that bring in large amounts of money; they are born speculators and often take any risk in any matter connected with money.
They have a remarkably elastic character. They quickly heal from the heavy blows of fate.
And it seems that nothing can affect them: if they are good by nature, they will remain good; if bad, then all the sermons in the world will not suffice to change them even slightly.

These people are guided only by a thirst for money. Every action is subordinated to the desire to make money. A strange trait of their spiritual life is that even their sexual desire is coloured by a love of money and success in business.
For them, sexual attraction is proportional to the wealth possessed by the object of their desire.
A woman or man who is physically unattractive will be desired, if they are wealthy.
Of course, they desire those who are beautiful, but if such a person has no material wealth, then the attraction is much less. This is characteristic only of people born on the 5th.
Just as people of the number 2 need the intellect of a partner, the 5 needs the partner to be wealthy, otherwise nothing good will come of it.
For such a person, physical beauty alone is not enough; it must necessarily be accompanied by wealth.

Though such people can quickly rally after any blows of fate, they are completely demoralised if they lose their money or their business.
Unfortunately, they repeat their mistakes, do not learn from experience, and easily yield to the temptation to make money and stake everything on it.
They are incorrigible gamblers, whether at the table, on the stock exchange, or at the racetrack. They cannot resist a large jackpot. Another negative trait is their high excitability, and if it is not softened sufficiently by their good qualities—intellect and logic—they completely lose all sense of proportion and suffer failure.

People born on the 5th are easy to get on with. They are gentle, diplomatic, and accommodating.
But these traits are subordinated to a thirst for money and come to the surface only if the goal is the acquisition of wealth.
Therefore the people around them soon feel revulsion at their mercenary nature. Only those who are themselves mercenary can understand them.
On the surface the number 5 gets on with everyone, but in reality such people are in harmony only with equally mercenary people and best of all with the number 5.
Sexually they are selfish, but here too they are ruled by a love of money.
A partner who is not wealthy will hardly attract them. On the other hand, the presence of money may very well make a person sexually attractive to them, despite physical unattractiveness.

Diamond, brilliant set in silver or platinum. (And it is better to wear it on the body.)
white metal, silver or platinum. On no account should they wear yellow gold. All jewellery should be set in white gold or platinum.
light grey, white, silver, and all that gleam. But just as they easily get on with all people, so too they can wear any colours, though they had best avoid dark tones.

These people are great materialists, and all their thoughts and actions are subordinated to the desire to make money. The thirst for money is so great that they will go to any extreme in order to acquire wealth.
If this cannot be done by honest means, they will not hesitate to resort to dishonest ones and often fall victim to the law.
They can be ruthless in the pursuit of money and will spare no one who stands in their path.
They cannot lead a beggarly life. They must not let their material aspirations get out of control, otherwise this will lead to ruin.
They should look at money more calmly and not lose their composure in pursuit of it. They have a subtle mind, and they are pleasant to deal with, provided only that they are reasonable in their material views.
6:
It signifies the predominance of love and all that pertains to love and the opposite sex.
Such people have a magnetic personality and immense sexual attractiveness.
They may be physically imperfect, yet they can have a memorable appearance and possess phenomenal charm.
By their manners and bearing they draw people of the opposite sex to themselves, as a flame draws moths.
These people are romantics and idealists and become almost slaves to those they love. They are very sensitive to atmosphere and surround themselves with beautiful things, if they have the means.
The sufficiently wealthy can become patrons of the arts. A woman born on the 6th takes pride in her home and very much loves to entertain guests. She loves to adorn her home, sparing no effort to make it even more beautiful, and loves to invite guests.

All these people live a full emotional life. They are romantic by nature and idealists by inclination.
Their emotional and sexual attachments are carefully balanced, and they need both.
They love in equal measure with both heart and body. They are ardent lovers. Their love is born in the mind just as much as in the body.
They possess immense physical attractiveness for the opposite sex, and charm. This cannot be explained, it can only be felt.
They love nature and blossom under the influence of all that is beautiful. They respond strongly to music and are great aesthetes.
They are sincere in their attachment, because they long for an ideal and will never betray one who can rely on them.
They are decisive and firm in carrying out their decisions and can think clearly. Despite their idealism and romanticism, they are also practical and industrious in any undertaking.
This is one of the best numbers, because it possesses almost all the traits: temperament, individuality, practicality and romanticism, sincerity, attractiveness, and devotion—combinations close to the ideal.

If one born on the 6th is betrayed by a person they trust, they become vengeful, and their hatred is as great as their love.
In other words, such people go to extremes. Their hostility does not diminish with time, but may, on the contrary, intensify. They will fan this enmity to the last, with no thought for the consequences harmful to themselves.

Their magnetic personality and charm draw people to them. In relations with partners they need only one quality—the ability to control their temper.
It is not that they are bad or hot-tempered, but if they are provoked, they can go to extremes.

This is one of the very best numbers. Such people are sincere and firm, loving and loved. They are unforgettable.
All those around them must be caring and must value their qualities, not offend them without cause, because they are as bad as enemies as they are good as friends.
If angered, they lose control, and this weakness they must overcome. Then they will become the most pleasant of people, friends, and lovers.
7:
In a certain sense it resembles the number 2, but it is far more individual and with a stronger character. Those born on the 7th are independent by nature, individual in character, and original in mind.
By nature they are not materialists, but more often than others they enjoy wealth and material goods, because, without trying especially hard to make money, they acquire it thanks to their thoughts and inventiveness.
Thanks to their individuality and originality, they become famous writers, poets, artists.
Their unusualness in everything sets them apart into a special class, and a writer born on the 7th is sure to enjoy the highest success thanks to the novelty of their ideas, great originality, and individual manner of exposition.
Such people usually start their own enterprises and rarely settle into a chair inherited from their elders.
If some enterprise or business comes to them as an inheritance, they prefer that others run it, while they themselves start something new.
These people usually undergo many changes in their life and very often change their surroundings; they travel a great deal. They are philanthropists by nature, and if wealthy, make large donations to charity.
Their religion is humanity. And their love for their neighbour is almost spiritual, though they are not excessively religious in the ordinary sense of the word.
They have strong intuition, and they quickly sense the mood of others, which gives them a great advantage in relations with people, because, being a step ahead, they can foresee the reaction.

Those born on the 7th are very changeable and restless by nature. They therefore waver and are rarely attached to one person for a long time in the early period of their life.
Not infrequently a person born on the 7th enters into marriage more than once in search of the right person.
Such people are constantly seeking security and always thinking about the future.
Thanks to their changeability such people are very interesting and sexually exciting, because one can never know what they will do the next moment.
They love to change partners from time to time. Such deviations do not affect their lasting bonds.
Those married to such a person should pay no attention to minor infidelities, since they help satisfy the craving for change. These adventures make the spouse more interesting.

These people possess many attractive qualities, yet their changeability can do them great harm, if constancy does not run like a red thread through their life.
If they dash about from one to another, it is only worse for them. Change is good, but one must have a firm anchor, otherwise nothing good will come of it.
Those around them must act so that the partner's wavering can always be prevented. One born on the 7th needs an anchor, and is constantly searching for it.
One should pay no attention to infidelity and not make a great fuss about it. The sexual partner must be strong and active, because such people have a great appetite. They have no inhibitions, and the partner must match this.
Moonstone, cat's eye, pearl (and it is better to wear them on the body).
Pink, all shades of green, yellow, and white colours.
Digestive organs, as well as nervous stress.

The number 7 is a very good number, possessing mysterious powers. These people are gifted with intuitive knowledge of their fellows, very creative and inventive. Their only shortcoming is a love of change, which can carry them to extremes.
They must be sure that the change they want is truly reasonable, otherwise they may do themselves harm.
Otherwise they are charming people, and they are easy to deal with. No one ever knows what tomorrow holds for such a person, and this element of surprise makes them simply delightful.
People of the number 7 are usually wealthy or else able to improve their well-being, and most often they live better in the second half of life than in the first. On the whole this is a good number, and people of the number 7 are fortunate.
8:
If one looks at the number itself, one can see that it consists of two circles and can be divided into two parts, which signifies a split personality, with both parts directly opposed to one another.
But both parts are connected like two sides of a coin. It depends on circumstances which of them plays the greater role.
In everyday life they also display a split personality, showing now one side, now the other.
Therefore such people are rarely understood by others, since if one side is comprehensible, the other is not, and one of the sides always remains mysterious.
Finding no bosom friends, they are very lonely, and being nonconformists, they usually get the worst of everything heaped upon their heads.
Labels are usually stuck on them; they have a reputation they do not in fact deserve, owing to the fact that their actions are misunderstood. These people have materialistic and spiritual leanings in equal measure.
Moreover, they possess these qualities simultaneously, and unless one understands that their personality is split, their words and actions will always be subject to distortion, to the point of nonsense.
These people are maximalists, and if the materialistic side of their nature comes to the fore, then immense success awaits them, since they are intelligent and can work very industriously.
But if they do not have brilliant success, then on the contrary they suffer terrible failure, because there is rarely anything in between for them. Because of their split nature and the failure of others to understand them, they may fall victim to persecution.
Being deep people, they perceive such persecution as torture, and very often in despair they may rebel. This worsens their position still further, since if they cannot find someone to punish, they punish themselves.
Among them there is a very high percentage of suicides, not because such is their natural inclination, but as a result of persecution.
If they do not punish themselves, they punish others and become an antisocial personality. Being extremists, they stop at nothing in their revenge. The results of this are easy to foresee.

People of the number 8 are very strong characters, capable of deep emotional attachment, but they often find themselves betrayed, because they are difficult to understand and to love.
They may seem cold while being warm-hearted in fact, since they do not know how to show their feelings.
They can express their feelings only through actions, which those around them usually misunderstand.
They are capable of great sacrifice, and they are often exploited by those to whom they are attached. This brings them great unhappiness, for if they love, they deny the object of their love nothing and as a result give more than they receive.
They possess a magnetic personality and are very attractive to the opposite sex.
They have great sexual power and activity, but possessing also spiritual leanings, they seek a spiritual bond with their partner, otherwise the relationship will not last.
The partner must be equally strong, since, not receiving satisfaction, such people seek it elsewhere. They need both in equal measure, and so they rarely find a suitable match.

In the world in which we live, the number 8 is not very suitable for birth. Though these people are very capable, they are rarely taken as such and are often given a label.
They are sincere, but persecution can drive them to self-destruction. They love deeply and are shattered when they are betrayed.
They fall into extremes and may today work furiously, while tomorrow they wallow in idleness.
They can be extremely vengeful if their hatred is provoked, and at times the rage of a boar may take hold of them.
They swing from extreme joy to extreme dejection. They lack balance and stability.
Such a split characterises their personality, and there will always exist in them the very opposite of what is visible on the outside.
Those who deal with them must always remember this. If now they are loving and kind, then at another time they are to the same degree hating and cruel.

A rare person can live in harmony with them. Such a person must possess deep understanding and the ability to respect the highly individual character of their partner.
These people need complete freedom, for independence is the chief thing for them.
One might say that few, or indeed none, can fully satisfy a person of the number 8. Therefore they search for a match all their life and very rarely find one. The only suitable option is a person of the same kind.
Those living beside them must maintain spiritual communion, to say nothing of the physical side.

People of the 8 are remarkable people, but they must remember that they will hardly ever be understood. In their own interest they should give up expecting approval from others, since they fall into extremes.
They should try to govern themselves and not let the pendulum of their emotions swing from one end to the other.
They need to direct their abilities towards a single goal, rather than dissipating them on useless emotions.
If they learn to accept what comes to them, without fighting the whole world, they will be far happier and able to pursue their goals more effectively.
They should develop patience and strictly balance determination and persistence. Only then will they rise high, possessing all that is necessary for it.
They are intelligent, industrious, possess great spiritual and mental strength, are artistic and sensitive. They need only to refrain from extremes.
9:
They are firm, do not lose heart in the face of the most cruel resistance. Such steadfastness gives them the ability to carry through to the end what they have begun.
They are ambitious and, since they foresee everything in advance, they usually attain their goal.
They have a dominant nature and an instinct for leadership. They do not like to submit, and are happy when they command.
Like the number 1, they can be rough with subordinates and are often deaf to the feelings of others. In the army they rise to the highest rank, even if they are not very popular. Nonetheless, they are respected for their abilities and strength of character.

These people are very sexual, but unable to express their desires. Therefore they often suffer failures. The expression of desire seems offensive to them. They consider it a weakness and may therefore drive themselves into deep despair instead of satisfying their desire.
These people are leaders in any matter, except sex; here they must be guided in such a way that it seems to them not that they are being yielded to, but that they are yielding to another.
This peculiarity must be reckoned with. Sometimes it may seem that they are cold and have no desire, but this is not so.
The desire is there, but at the same time there is a feeling that to express desire is to lose one's dignity.
Emotionally these are deep people; they do not fall in love or become friends very easily. But if it does happen, they are friends for life.
Their high sexuality often leads them astray, because with a great force of physical attraction logic retreats into the background.
Their first marriage often breaks down, since they mistakenly take physical attraction for love, and after the satisfaction of desire feel nothing at all.
But they rarely make the same mistake twice. This applies not only to marriage; in practice it applies to all matters. Therefore they are successful in life, since they are intelligent enough to learn not only from their own mistakes but from those of others.

These are dominant people who do not reckon with others. Therefore they have many enemies.
But they also attain success, which they owe to themselves alone, since they are industrious and persistent natures.
But having attained success, they rest on their laurels and consider themselves above others. They are intolerant of those who disagree with them. This is an innate intolerance.

These people can get on with those who are their spiritual equals. They possess strong analytical thinking and seek people with an equally keen mind.
They prefer to surround themselves with people of good reputation.
They do not acknowledge authorities, and will not trust until they have made sure of a person's qualities.
Only then will they accept the person fully and become a friend for life. They do not like whiners. Weakness is something they never tolerate.
All shades of red, scarlet, pink
Fever of any kind, as well as smallpox, mumps. These people will undergo an operation at least once in their life.

The number 9 is a bold number, and people of the 9 are courageous people. But they can be cruel if angered, and may speak without thinking, thereby acquiring enemies.
They need to learn to govern themselves and their speech. They easily seek out and find trouble heaped on their own heads through thoughtlessness, and expend much time and energy on solving the problems they have created.
This number is good, but its possessor should develop within themselves at least a little restraint and tolerance in relations with people, otherwise one may end up isolated, without friends.
These are very capable people who rise to a high rung in their career; they need only try not to step on the toes of others in doing so.
Men should beware of becoming infatuated with unworthy women, since a cunning schemer can easily lead them to a sorrowful end.
They need to show natural restraint and self-control in relations with the opposite sex. The second half of their life is more fortunate.
As soon as they overcome the troubles at the beginning of their life, they attain success and tranquillity. The main thing is to hold one's tongue.
